prompts.prompt: What is the overall health of my Thread network right now?
What looks most risky in my Thread network right now?
Do you see evidence of a live partition split or two active Thread networks right now? If not, say that clearly.
Do you see any chokepoints in my Thread network right now? If not, say that clearly.
Which links look weak or error-prone right now?
Tell me what is going on with node e6684b9903e8970f.
Has Family Room Track Lights been changing parents or partitions recently?
Which offline or stale nodes should I investigate first?
Why does my sleepy end device keep dropping off the network?
A new Matter device will not commission onto Thread. What should I check first?
Why would commissioning time out even though the border router is online?
Could stale credentials or an old dataset explain why a device cannot join Thread?
Why is the same physical device showing up under multiple EUI64s?
Which routers look like the most important path to the border router right now?
Do retry or frame-error counters suggest RF problems right now?
Did the Thread channel change recently, and if so what evidence supports that?
What changed in the topology over the last 24 hours?
Looking at the past 3 days, tell me about the most recent devices added to the network and whether any device found a new and better path to the OTBR.
How has my network changed in the past 3 days?
Which devices have re-attached recently, and does that suggest instability?
Would adding or moving a router likely improve this Thread mesh?
Does the current evidence point to a border-router problem or a mesh problem?
Is my Thread data fresh enough to trust right now, or is the pipeline stale?
Does this look like a full mesh outage, a border-router outage, or just a few devices dropping off?
Which node shows the strongest signs of parent flapping or attachment instability right now?
Do any nodes look like they are in a retry storm right now, and which one looks worst?
Is there evidence of stale routing entries or half-dead paths in the current mesh state?
Do I have evidence of a phantom router or ghost node in the mesh right now?
Has the Thread leader or router-role assignment been churning recently?
Does this look more like an mDNS or SRP discovery problem than a Thread mesh problem?
Does any duplicate device identity look like a stale re-commissioned ghost instead of a real second device?
Could stale route-table state explain what looks like a partition or a bad path to the border router?
